old_claim.note: Leather Tanning provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct tanning dependency remains because a hide-piercing bone tool can be manufactured independently of any particular hide-treatment process.

source_shape.source_locator: The review treats bone awls as hide-piercing implements and discusses sewing technologies without making leather tanning a manufacturing prerequisite for the tool.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source distinguishes the piercing implement from the hide-processing and clothing applications in which it may be used.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Use on hide does not make a specific tanning process causally necessary for manufacturing the awl, while bone_tool_making preserves the actual artifact mechanism.

ontology_before: A use context, processed leather, was modeled as an enabling prerequisite for manufacturing a bone awl.
ontology_after: Bone awls depend on bone-tool manufacture; hide preparation remains an application context rather than a direct prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: leather_tanning is no longer a direct prerequisite for bone_awls_needles.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to a documented toolkit whose production process explicitly required chemically tanned leather.
A direct archaeological source established tanning as necessary to manufacture the scoped awl artifacts.
short_reason: Hide use does not make tanning a tool-manufacturing dependency.
